useCases.title: Unplanned Downtime Root-Cause Agent
statusQuo: Downtime events are logged in the MES with generic reason codes that operators pick under time pressure, so the top loss category is 'Other'.
Engineers manually export historian trends and MES logs into spreadsheets days after an event to reconstruct what happened.
Recurring failure patterns across shifts and lines go unnoticed because nobody correlates events across data silos.
agentification: Listens for downtime events from Opcenter MES and automatically pulls the surrounding sensor window from the PI historian into BigQuery.
Correlates the event against historical failure signatures and drafts a ranked root-cause hypothesis with supporting trend charts.
Escalates repeat offenders to the plant manager and creates a follow-up action item with the evidence package attached.

useCases.title: Production Schedule Adherence Monitor
statusQuo: The scheduler reconciles SAP planned orders against MES confirmations in a spreadsheet every morning, an exercise that takes two hours.
Orders slipping behind plan are only discovered when a customer service rep calls asking where the shipment is.
Re-sequencing decisions are made from gut feel because the true capacity impact of a slip is never quantified.
agentification: Runs every shift to compare SAP S/4HANA PP planned orders against actual MES confirmations landed in BigQuery.
Flags orders trending late, quantifies the downstream capacity and customer impact, and recommends a re-sequencing option.
Publishes a shift-start adherence dashboard in Looker and notifies the scheduler of the top three at-risk orders.

useCases.title: OEE Loss Pareto Analyzer

kpis.label: Time to build a loss Pareto

statusQuo: OEE is reported as a single monthly number with no drill-down, so availability, performance, and quality losses are argued about in meetings.
Building a loss Pareto for one line requires a week of manual MES data extraction and cleansing.
Improvement projects are chosen by whoever argues loudest rather than by quantified loss buckets.
agentification: Decomposes OEE into availability, performance, and quality losses per line, shift, and SKU from MES data in BigQuery on request.
Generates a ranked loss Pareto with dollarized impact and highlights the single biggest addressable loss bucket.
Recommends candidate kaizen targets and publishes the analysis to Looker for the improvement team.

useCases.title: Changeover Time Optimization Agent
statusQuo: Changeover durations vary wildly between crews and nobody knows which sequence tricks the fastest crew uses.
The production sequence from SAP ignores changeover families, so the line runs light-to-dark-to-light color swaps back to back.
Supervisors find out a changeover blew past standard only when the whole shift output number comes in short.
agentification: Analyzes every changeover recorded in Opcenter MES weekly and benchmarks duration by crew, product family, and sequence.
Recommends changeover-family-aware sequencing adjustments to the SAP S/4HANA PP planned order sequence.
Notifies the supervisor in real time when a live changeover exceeds 120% of standard so help can be routed to the line.

useCases.title: Scrap and Rework Analytics Engine
statusQuo: Scrap is booked in SAP at month-end as a lump cost variance with no link back to the machine, shift, or lot that produced it.
The controller chases production leads by email for scrap explanations, and answers arrive after the close.
Rework loops are invisible because reworked units re-enter the line under the same order number.
agentification: Joins MES defect confirmations with SAP S/4HANA PP order costs in BigQuery nightly to attribute scrap dollars to machine, shift, material lot, and operator.
Detects scrap spikes against a rolling baseline and drafts a variance explanation with the top contributing factors.
Publishes a daily scrap cost dashboard in Looker and escalates any single-day spike above threshold to the plant controller and production supervisor.

useCases.title: SPC Drift Detection Monitor

kpis.label: Out-of-control conditions caught before defects ship
Manual control chart reviews per week

statusQuo: Quality engineers eyeball dozens of control charts once a day, so a process that drifts at 2 a.m. runs out of spec for hours.
Western Electric rule violations are detected inconsistently because each engineer applies the rules by memory.
By the time an inspection lot fails in SAP QM, an entire batch has already been produced against a drifting process.
agentification: Streams inspection results from SAP QM and in-line measurements from MES into BigQuery and evaluates every point against SPC run rules in near real time.
Distinguishes true process drift from measurement noise using historical process capability baselines per characteristic.
Notifies the quality engineer and line lead the moment a drift signature emerges and recommends whether to hold the affected lots.

useCases.title: CAPA Orchestration Agent
statusQuo: CAPA tasks live in email threads and a shared spreadsheet, so owners forget actions until the audit two weeks before.
Root-cause sections are written from scratch every time even when a nearly identical CAPA was closed last year.
Effectiveness checks are a checkbox exercise because nobody re-queries defect data after closure.
agentification: Opens a structured CAPA workspace when a qualifying notification is raised in SAP QM, drafts the problem statement, and proposes similar historical CAPAs from BigQuery.
Creates and routes containment, root-cause, and corrective-action tasks in ServiceNow with due dates, and escalates stalled tasks to the quality manager.
Runs post-closure effectiveness checks by monitoring defect rates for the affected characteristic and reopens the CAPA if the failure mode recurs.

useCases.title: Supplier Quality Scorecard Engine
statusQuo: Quarterly supplier scorecards are assembled by hand from SAP QM inspection lots and MM receipts, and half the quarter is over before they ship.
PPM, on-time delivery, and 8D responsiveness live in different reports, so no one sees a supplier's full risk picture.
Supplier review meetings run on anecdotes because the data is six weeks stale.
agentification: Refreshes supplier PPM, lot rejection rate, deviation history, and 8D closure speed weekly from SAP QM and SAP S/4HANA MM data in BigQuery.
Generates a ranked risk scorecard per supplier and drafts the quarterly business review pack automatically.
Recommends which suppliers need a formal improvement plan and publishes trend dashboards to Looker for the sourcing team.

useCases.title: Nonconformance Triage Agent
statusQuo: Every nonconformance notification lands in one queue and the shift quality lead reads each one to decide who should handle it.
Material sits in the MRB cage for days awaiting disposition while planners scramble to cover the shortage it created.
Similar defects get different dispositions depending on which engineer picks up the ticket.
agentification: Classifies each new SAP QM nonconformance by defect type, severity, and affected order using the notification text and linked MES genealogy.
Routes the notification to the correct disposition owner and suggests a disposition based on how materially identical cases were resolved.
Flags NCs blocking shippable orders and escalates them to the shift quality lead ahead of the queue.

useCases.title: Batch Record Review Analyzer
statusQuo: Reviewers page through hundreds of electronic batch record entries checking each value against limits by eye.
Ninety-five percent of review time is spent confirming records that have no exceptions at all.
Deviations discovered late in review restart the correction loop and push the release date out by days.
agentification: Screens the full batch record against specifications, procedure limits, and e-signature completeness the moment production closes the order in MES.
Generates a review-by-exception summary listing only the entries that deviate, with links to the source records in SAP QM.
Drafts the release recommendation for clean batches so the quality systems lead reviews exceptions instead of paperwork.

useCases.title: Predictive Asset Failure Monitor
statusQuo: Vibration and temperature trends sit in the PI historian, but nobody looks at them until a bearing has already seized.
Condition monitoring rounds happen monthly on a clipboard, so a fault that develops in week one runs three more weeks.
The same pump fails the same way every year and the pattern lives only in one technician's memory.
agentification: Continuously scores critical-asset sensor streams from the PI System in BigQuery against learned degradation signatures.
Raises an early-warning alert with estimated remaining useful life when an asset's signature departs from its healthy baseline.
Creates a prioritized condition-based work order in IBM Maximo with the suspect component, evidence trends, and recommended repair window.

useCases.title: PM Schedule Optimization Engine

kpis.label: PM labor hours with no findings

statusQuo: PM intervals were set from OEM manuals a decade ago and have never been revisited against actual failure history.
Technicians grease and inspect healthy equipment while assets that actually fail sit on the same calendar cadence.
The planner has no way to see which PM tasks have never once produced a finding.
agentification: Cross-references every PM task in IBM Maximo against failure history and runtime data from the PI System in BigQuery quarterly.
Recommends interval extensions for zero-finding PMs and tightened or condition-based triggers for failure-prone assets, with the evidence attached.
Drafts the updated PM route packages for the planner's approval and tracks failure rates after each change to validate it.

useCases.title: Spare Parts Stockout Prediction Agent
statusQuo: Min/max levels were set once at commissioning and never adjusted, so fast movers stock out while obsolete parts gather dust.
A critical repair stalls for two weeks because the one bearing on the shelf turned out to be reserved against another work order.
The storeroom manager discovers a lead-time change only when the expedite quote comes back at triple the price.
agentification: Forecasts part-level demand from IBM Maximo work order history, upcoming PM schedules, and asset criticality in BigQuery.
Predicts stockout risk against on-hand and on-order quantities in SAP S/4HANA MM, accounting for current supplier lead times.
Recommends reorder point changes and drafts purchase requisitions for at-risk critical spares before the shelf goes empty.

useCases.title: Work Order Backlog Triage Orchestrator
statusQuo: The backlog holds 800 open work orders and the supervisor triages them by scrolling and instinct every Monday.
Duplicate requests for the same failing asset are dispatched to different technicians on different days.
High-criticality safety work sits behind cosmetic requests because every ticket enters the queue at the same priority.
agentification: Scores the entire IBM Maximo backlog weekly on asset criticality, safety impact, age, and parts availability from BigQuery.
Merges duplicate requests, bundles work orders on the same asset into single visits, and drafts the weekly schedule proposal.
Escalates safety-critical orders aging past threshold to the supervisor through ServiceNow and tracks them to closure.

useCases.title: Bad Actor Asset Analyzer

kpis.label: Maintenance cost on top-10 bad actors

statusQuo: Identifying the worst-performing assets means exporting a year of Maximo history and pivoting it in a spreadsheet for two weeks.
Cost, downtime, and failure-count rankings disagree with each other, so every reliability review reopens the same debate.
Defect-elimination projects launch without failure-mode data and end up treating symptoms.
agentification: Ranks assets on a combined index of repair cost, downtime contribution, and failure frequency from IBM Maximo and PI System data in BigQuery on request.
Clusters each bad actor's work order text into dominant failure modes and summarizes the recurring mechanism.
Generates a defect-elimination candidate briefing with projected savings and publishes the ranking dashboard to Looker.

useCases.title: Material Shortage Early Warning Monitor
statusQuo: Shortages surface when a picker reports an empty bin, hours before the line needs the part.
The planner manually walks MRP exception messages in SAP every morning and triages a few hundred by instinct.
Supplier delay notifications arrive by email and rarely make it into the plan before the impact hits.
agentification: Projects material coverage daily by netting Kinaxis RapidResponse supply plans against SAP S/4HANA MM inventory, open POs, and confirmed supplier dates in BigQuery.
Ranks emerging shortages by production impact and identifies which specific orders and lines each one will stop.
Notifies the supply planner with a resolution option per shortage — expedite, substitute, or reschedule — and drafts the expedite request when chosen.

useCases.title: Supplier Delivery Risk Analyzer

kpis.label: POs flagged at risk before due date
Safety stock held against unreliable suppliers

statusQuo: On-time delivery is reported monthly in arrears, so a supplier's slide shows up in the metrics six weeks after it started hurting the plant.
Buyers chase overdue POs one by one with no view of which supplier's slippage actually threatens production.
Safety stocks are padded uniformly because nobody quantifies delivery risk per supplier.
agentification: Scores every open purchase order's late-delivery probability from the supplier's rolling performance, order size, and lead-time trend in BigQuery.
Simulates the production impact of predicted slips through Kinaxis RapidResponse and highlights the POs worth chasing this week.
Publishes supplier reliability trend dashboards in Looker and recommends differentiated safety stock levels to the materials manager.

useCases.title: Inventory Rebalancing Orchestrator
statusQuo: One plant expedites a component at premium cost while a sister plant sits on eight months of the same part.
Rebalancing opportunities are found once a quarter through a manual multi-site spreadsheet reconciliation.
Transfer orders take days to arrange because each one requires an email negotiation between site planners.
agentification: Scans multi-site stock positions and projected demand in Kinaxis RapidResponse and SAP S/4HANA MM data in BigQuery every night for surplus-deficit pairs.
Recommends specific inter-site transfers with freight cost versus expedite cost trade-offs quantified.
Drafts the stock transport orders in SAP for approval and tracks each transfer through receipt.

useCases.title: Expedite Request Triage Agent
statusQuo: Expedite requests arrive as emails and hallway conversations, and buyers process them first-come-first-served.
Half the expedites turn out to be unnecessary because the need date moved or stock existed at another location.
Nobody can report what expediting actually costs the business each quarter.
agentification: Intakes every expedite request through ServiceNow, validates the true requirement date against the Kinaxis RapidResponse plan, and checks alternate stock in SAP S/4HANA MM.
Rejects unnecessary requests with the evidence attached and ranks genuine ones by production impact and cost.
Drafts the supplier expedite communication for approved requests and logs cost and outcome to BigQuery for quarterly reporting.

useCases.title: Demand-Supply Gap Scenario Engine
statusQuo: Each what-if question from the executive S&OP meeting takes the planning team most of a week to model.
Demand-supply gaps are presented as aggregate units, hiding which customers and SKUs actually absorb the shortfall.
Scenario assumptions live in personal spreadsheets that disagree with the system of record.
agentification: Runs demand-shift, capacity-loss, and supplier-disruption scenarios through Kinaxis RapidResponse on request and lands results in BigQuery for comparison.
Translates each gap into affected customers, SKUs, revenue, and margin rather than aggregate units.
Generates the executive S&OP briefing with side-by-side scenario trade-offs and publishes it to Looker before the meeting.

useCases.title: ECO Impact Analysis Agent

kpis.label: Obsolete inventory written off per change

statusQuo: Assessing a change means manually tracing where-used across Windchill BOMs, then separately checking SAP for open orders and on-hand stock.
Effectivity dates are picked without knowing how much inventory the change will strand.
Downstream teams learn about a released change when a part stops fitting on the line.
agentification: Traces full where-used impact across PTC Windchill PLM product structures the moment an ECO enters review, joined with open orders, WIP, and inventory from SAP S/4HANA PP in BigQuery.
Recommends the effectivity date that minimizes stranded inventory and open-order disruption, with the cost trade-off quantified.
Drafts the impact summary for the change board and notifies affected planners, buyers, and line engineers on release.

useCases.title: BOM PLM-ERP Sync Monitor

kpis.label: BOM discrepancies between PLM and ERP

statusQuo: Windchill and SAP BOMs drift apart through failed interface transfers and manual ERP edits that nobody reconciles.
Discrepancies are found by periodic manual audits that sample a fraction of the part universe.
A stale ERP BOM triggers wrong-part picks that surface as assembly-line stoppages weeks later.
agentification: Compares released PTC Windchill PLM structures against SAP S/4HANA PP production BOMs in BigQuery every night, item by item.
Classifies each mismatch by cause — failed transfer, manual override, or pending change — and routes it to the right owner with fix instructions.
Escalates discrepancies on parts with imminent planned orders so they are corrected before a build starts.

useCases.title: NPI Launch Readiness Orchestrator

kpis.label: Deliverables discovered missing at gate review

statusQuo: Launch readiness is tracked in a 400-row spreadsheet that is out of date the day after each program review.
Missing PPAP documents, unreleased drawings, and open tooling tasks surface at the gate review instead of weeks before.
The program manager spends two days per gate chasing status from twelve functions by email.
agentification: Continuously reconciles gate deliverables against actual state — released drawings and documents in PTC Windchill PLM, task completion in Jira, and tooling and qualification records in BigQuery.
Predicts gate readiness from current burn-down trends and flags deliverables that will miss the date while there is still time to recover.
Generates the gate review readiness pack automatically and escalates critical-path blockers to the accountable function lead.

useCases.title: Engineering Change Backlog Analyzer

kpis.label: Open change requests older than 90 days

statusQuo: Hundreds of open change requests sit in Windchill with no ranking, so the change board reviews them in submission order.
Duplicate and overlapping requests against the same part consume separate review cycles.
Cost-reduction changes worth six figures wait behind typo-level drawing corrections.
agentification: Clusters the open change request backlog in PTC Windchill PLM by affected part and problem description, merging duplicates and linking related Jira issues.
Scores each request on cost impact, quality risk, and affected production volume from BigQuery data.
Drafts a prioritized change board agenda with a one-paragraph summary per request so the board decides instead of deciphering.

useCases.title: Component Obsolescence Risk Monitor
statusQuo: End-of-life notices arrive in a shared inbox and are matched to product BOMs by hand, weeks later, if at all.
Last-time-buy windows close before anyone calculates how many units of remaining demand the part covers.
A discontinued component is often discovered when a purchase order bounces back unfulfilled.
agentification: Cross-references supplier end-of-life and lifecycle-risk signals against active PTC Windchill PLM BOM usage and remaining demand from SAP S/4HANA MM in BigQuery weekly.
Quantifies exposure per affected product — remaining demand, current stock, and last-time-buy quantity required.
Notifies the component engineer with a recommended action per part — last-time buy, qualify an alternate, or launch a redesign — and creates the tracking task for the chosen path.

useCases.title: Permit-to-Work Compliance Monitor

kpis.label: High-risk work performed without valid permit
Expired permits caught before work continues

statusQuo: Hot work and confined-space permits are checked against actual work activity only during spot walkdowns.
Permits expire mid-job and crews keep working because nobody tracks the clock across dozens of active permits.
Contractor permits are filed on paper, making audit preparation a multi-day scavenger hunt.
agentification: Cross-checks every active high-risk work order against valid permits in Sphera EHS in real time, including isolation and gas-test prerequisites.
Notifies the area supervisor and EHS manager the moment work is detected against a missing, expired, or mismatched permit.
Generates the complete permit compliance evidence pack on demand and logs every exception to BigQuery for trend review.

useCases.title: Incident and Near-Miss Triage Agent
statusQuo: Near-miss reports pile up unread for a week because the safety officer triages them between audits and training sessions.
Serious-injury-potential events get the same cursory handling as a paper cut because severity screening is inconsistent.
Corrective actions from investigations drift past due dates with no systematic follow-up.
agentification: Classifies every new Sphera EHS report by severity potential and regulatory reportability within minutes of submission.
Routes serious-injury-potential events to immediate investigation, drafts the initial investigation record, and proposes similar historical events from BigQuery.
Creates corrective-action tasks in ServiceNow and escalates any action aging past its due date to the responsible manager.

useCases.title: Energy Intensity Monitoring Engine
statusQuo: Energy performance is reviewed monthly from utility bills, long after a stuck valve or idling compressor has burned weeks of waste.
Consumption is not normalized per unit produced, so efficiency losses hide inside volume swings.
Weekend and idle-hour baseload creep goes unchallenged because nobody compares shifts against a baseline.
agentification: Computes energy intensity per line and product daily by joining PI System meter data with production output in BigQuery.
Detects consumption anomalies against weather- and volume-normalized baselines and identifies the likely equipment source.
Publishes intensity trend dashboards in Looker and recommends the top three conservation actions with quantified savings to the sustainability lead.

useCases.title: Regulatory Emissions Reporting Agent
statusQuo: Quarterly emissions reports are compiled by hand from CEMS exports, fuel logs, and production records across a dozen spreadsheets.
A permit limit exceedance is often discovered during report preparation, weeks after it happened.
Each report cycle rediscovers the same data gaps and unit-conversion errors under deadline pressure.
agentification: Aggregates continuous emissions data from the PI System with fuel and production records in BigQuery, validated daily against permit limits in Sphera EHS.
Notifies the compliance specialist immediately when any rolling-average parameter trends toward a permit threshold.
Drafts the complete regulatory submission with calculation lineage attached, ready for review and sign-off.

useCases.title: Safety Observation Trend Analyzer
statusQuo: Thousands of behavior-based safety observations are collected each year and read by no one beyond a raw count on a slide.
Emerging risk patterns — the same unsafe condition reported in three departments — are invisible in the quarterly rollup.
Toolbox talk topics are recycled from a stale list instead of reflecting what observers are actually reporting.
agentification: Mines Sphera EHS observation free-text weekly in BigQuery to cluster emerging unsafe conditions and behaviors by area, shift, and task type.
Correlates observation clusters with incident history to surface the leading indicators that historically precede recordables.
Drafts targeted toolbox talk content for the highest-risk clusters and publishes the trend dashboard to Looker for area supervisors.
